name: Mike Thompson
occupation: Senior Product Manager at Vertex Health (hybrid, 3 days in office)
lifeContext: Mike lives in Frisco, Texas with his wife Sarah (34, freelance UX designer), their son Ethan (8, 3rd grade at Liberty Elementary), and daughter Lily (3). They moved from Chicago 2 years ago for Mike's PM job at Vertex Health in Plano, commuting Mon/Wed/Fri. Ethan plays soccer (Frisco FC U-9) coached by Mike, and takes piano from Mrs. Patterson. Lily is in daycare and takes swim lessons on Saturdays. Mike's parents, Tom (68, retired teacher) and Linda (65, retired librarian), live in Naperville, IL. Sarah's mom Patricia (62, retired nurse) lives in Austin; her dad Robert passed away from cancer 4 years ago. Robert's woodworking memory is cherished. At work, Mike manages the CareConnect patient portal launch, mentoring junior PM Anika Patel and managing conflicts with VP of Engineering Greg Holloway. The family is planning a Colorado road trip, considering adopting a golden retriever puppy, and refinancing their mortgage. Sarah is launching an online design course. Neighbors Dave and Kim Nguyen, and their son Marcus, are close friends. College buddy Jake Morrison visits yearly. The home uses Jarvis (OpenClaw AI) as the central assistant.
companionRelationship: Mike has used Jarvis (his local OpenClaw AI assistant) for 4 months as a personal assistant, thinking partner, and family command center. He asks Jarvis for shopping lists, appointment reminders, and to manage the family calendar, while also brainstorming work presentations and product strategy. He values Jarvis remembering context across conversations, like ongoing projects and preferences. Mike starts mornings with a greeting and logs a journal entry in the evening.
